video_title: Crypto is Bottoming. We’re About to Go Much Higher.
gist: A crypto trader argues the real cycle top was July 2025 (not the October $126K Bitcoin ATH), so the market is likely in a bottoming process, and explains why he's overweight perp-DEX tokens HYPE and LIT (betting on real-world-asset perps beating options), and why he bought back into Zcash after a 60% crash tied to an Orchard pool exploit scare.

insights.claim: The real euphoric peak of this crypto cycle was arguably July 2025, when Bitcoin first hit $125K and Ethereum ran to $5,000 on DAT-mania buying (e.g., Bitmine), rather than October 2025's technical all-time high of $126K, which was a muted, non-euphoric top that didn't spark an alt season.
insights.speaker: Taiki Maeda
insights.so_what: If the real top was July 2025, the market has already endured roughly a year of bear-market pain, implying a bottoming process is further along than consensus (which fixates on the October top) assumes.
insights.quote: if you view it from this lens and the real euphoria happened in July then we've already been in a one-year bare market

insights.claim: He no longer tries to fight or predict the classic four-year Bitcoin cycle in the short term; instead he positions based on a time-and-price framework, arguing that even someone who expects new Q4 lows should still start slowly allocating now because a longer time horizon and conviction-building in specific narratives are his actual edge.
insights.speaker: Taiki Maeda
insights.so_what: Suggests dollar-cost-averaging into favored assets now rather than waiting for a confirmed bottom, since timing precision is not achievable.

insights.claim: Because so much capital is reportedly sitting on the sidelines waiting to buy a Q4 2026 bottom, he argues downside is limited and it may pay to front-run that crowd by reallocating into favored assets in mid-to-late Q3, which he says he has already started doing.
insights.speaker: Taiki Maeda
insights.so_what: A crowded consensus trade waiting for Q4 could mean the bottom never arrives at expected levels, rewarding early positioning ahead of the crowd.
insights.quote: there's so much capital waiting to buy in Q4 um that you know how much downside could there possibly be

insights.claim: Drawing on the 2022 cycle, he notes the bottoming process arguably began around June 2022 when Bitcoin fell to roughly $20K after a major crypto collapse, even though marginally lower prices came later, and market participants who allocated to strong assets during that uncertain window outperformed over the following 12-18 months.
insights.speaker: Taiki Maeda
insights.so_what: Historical precedent supports buying into strength/conviction during an uncertain bottoming phase rather than waiting for a confirmed final low.

themes.theme: Perp-DEX and real-world-asset perps bull case

insights.claim: He is overweight Hyperliquid (HYPE) and Lighter (LIT) partly because both have aggressively bought back their own tokens in 2026 — Hyperliquid roughly 3.4% of circulating supply year-to-date and Lighter roughly 6.3% — and both have decoupled from Bitcoin/ETH price weakness by hitting new all-time highs.
insights.speaker: Taiki Maeda
insights.so_what: Aggressive token buybacks funded by real protocol revenue signal fundamental strength independent of broader crypto beta, supporting continued overweight positioning.
insights.quote: Hyperlid uh has purchased roughly 3.4% of the circulating supply year to date. Lighter has done roughly 6.3%.

insights.claim: His core bull case for perp-DEX tokens is not further crypto-native adoption, which he views as largely saturated and already priced in, but the growth of real-world-asset (RWA) perps — gold, oil, equities — which he expects to become larger than crypto perps within roughly 6-12 months, plus traditional retail migrating from options to perps as a simpler leverage instrument.
insights.speaker: Taiki Maeda
insights.so_what: Positions perp-DEX platforms building out RWA markets (e.g., Hyperliquid's HIP-3) as the next growth leg for the sector, largely independent of crypto's own price action.
insights.quote: the next leg in crypto or perpex coins isn't going to come from crypto people buying, you know, perplex coins, right?

insights.claim: He holds a structurally bearish view on most crypto altcoins, believing most will go to zero because they lack fundamentals and cash flow and are purely speculative beta to Bitcoin/ETH — which is why he avoids crypto-only perp platforms in favor of ones expanding into real-world-asset markets.
insights.speaker: Taiki Maeda
insights.so_what: Reframes altcoin exposure away from broad-basket speculation toward infrastructure (perp DEXs) that captures fee/volume growth regardless of which individual altcoins survive.
insights.quote: I have a view that most crypto alts are going to go to zero.

insights.claim: Because HYPE and LIT's growth thesis now rests on real-world-asset perp volume rather than Bitcoin/ETH price, he would treat a drop in either token driven purely by BTC/ETH weakness as a buying opportunity rather than a reason to sell.
insights.speaker: Taiki Maeda
insights.so_what: Signals he'd add to HYPE/LIT on BTC-driven weakness, treating the correlation as a temporary mispricing rather than a fundamental risk.

insights.claim: On Variational, a perp platform he actively trades and farms, roughly 25% of open interest is now in traditional/real-world-asset markets and total open interest has reached roughly $1.2-1.3 billion, a trend he wants to see continue.
insights.speaker: Taiki Maeda
insights.so_what: Rising RWA share of OI on Variational is a leading indicator he's tracking to validate the broader RWA-perps growth thesis and justify continued airdrop farming there.
insights.quote: roughly 25% of their OI uh is in trai markets which I think is very very impressive

insights.claim: He estimates that if Variational eventually launches its token with a 25% airdrop at a roughly $1 billion fully diluted valuation, that would imply about $27 per farmed point, though he wouldn't be surprised by a $2-3 billion FDV launch depending on how HYPE and LIT perform; he expects the points program to end by Q3 2026 and a token generation event around year-end.
insights.speaker: Taiki Maeda
insights.so_what: Gives a rough dollar-per-point valuation benchmark for evaluating whether continued Variational farming is worth the effort/capital before the points program closes.
insights.quote: if they do like a 25% air drop and they launch at, let's say, a billion dollar valuation, that would put roughly $27 a point

insights.claim: He is shorting SUI on Variational as a hedge against his own spot portfolio (not as a directional bet, since he doesn't believe in SUI as a token), simultaneously earning airdrop farming points regardless of the trade's own P&L — an example of using speculative positions to add convexity to future returns via points programs.
insights.speaker: Taiki Maeda
insights.so_what: Illustrates a tactic of routing directional or hedge trades through a farmable platform so that either P&L outcome (profit or loss) still yields airdrop upside.
insights.quote: I'm like shorting sooie uh on variational against my portfolio

insights.claim: He fully exited Zcash near the lows in early June 2026 after fears of a potential 'infinite exploit' vulnerability in the Orchard shielded pool triggered a roughly 60% price crash, then rebought last week (mid-July 2026) once price action suggested the asset was recovering, arguing the recovery itself makes ZEC more 'anti-fragile' going forward.
insights.speaker: Taiki Maeda
insights.so_what: An asset that survives a credible existential FUD event and recovers tends to become structurally more resilient to future FUD, similar to how repeated 'Bitcoin is dead' calls strengthened Bitcoin's own narrative.
insights.quote: there was this uh this potential vulnerability in Orchard Pool...that led to the price of Zcash going down 60% in a straight line essentially

insights.claim: Zcash developers are rolling out a new shielded pool called Ironwood on July 28, 2026, intended to be quantum-resistant and to bring formal verification to the protocol in order to rule out undetectable bugs, which he views as a catalyst that will effectively demonstrate no exploit occurred and reduce the asset's tail risk.
insights.speaker: Taiki Maeda
insights.so_what: A concrete, dated catalyst (July 28 Ironwood launch) to watch for confirmation of the security narrative and potential price reaction.
insights.quote: the Zcash devs...are rolling out a new shielded pool called Ironwood, um, on July 28th, which will...make it, you know, uh, quantum proof

insights.claim: Zcash co-founder Zooko has reportedly used an Anthropic AI auditing tool (referred to in the video as 'Anthropic's Mythos') to help audit the protocol's code, part of a broader trend the speaker expects where formal verification and AI-assisted code auditing become table stakes for crypto projects.
insights.speaker: Taiki Maeda
insights.so_what: AI-assisted formal verification could become a differentiator and de-risking factor investors screen for across crypto protocols, not just Zcash.
insights.quote: Zuko who's like the founder of Zcash or one of the founders of Zcash has also used anthropics mythos uh to audit the protocol

insights.claim: He applies a Soros-style reflexivity framework to Zcash: repeated attempts to kill the asset (the Orchard pool FUD and prior crashes) followed by price recoveries progressively strengthen holder conviction and community belief, which he expects to trigger a broader 'narrative revival' once price fully recovers and even skeptics can no longer dismiss the asset.
insights.speaker: Taiki Maeda
insights.so_what: A full price recovery from the Orchard pool scare could be a signal to watch as a reflexive inflection point that draws in previously skeptical capital.
insights.quote: Soros theory of reflexivity suggests that you know uh the fundamentals will make price go up and price going up will change the perception of an asset

insights.claim: He has sold Bitcoin on two separate occasions (roughly one and two months prior) to buy more Zcash, framing it as a bet on the Zcash/Bitcoin market-cap ratio rising rather than a bet that Zcash will outright replace Bitcoin — he compares the pairing to gold and silver, where owning both makes sense given their different risk/narrative exposures (privacy, quantum-resistance, and the Saylor/Strategy overhang on Bitcoin).
insights.speaker: Taiki Maeda
insights.so_what: Frames ZEC as a portfolio diversifier against Bitcoin-specific tail risks rather than a competing bet, suggesting a barbell allocation (e.g., 90% BTC / 10% ZEC) instead of an all-or-nothing stance.
insights.quote: I've actually sold some Bitcoin uh to to buy Zcash. Um I did that last month. I did that two months ago. I'm doing more uh right now.

themes.theme: Bitcoin overhang risks: Saylor and quantum computing

insights.claim: Michael Saylor's Strategy has been selling Bitcoin to fund its Stretch (STRC) preferred-stock product and build cash reserves, and after roughly six years as a consistent net Bitcoin buyer, Saylor may become a persistent net seller going forward, which the speaker views as creating genuine execution risk for Bitcoin's price.
insights.speaker: Taiki Maeda
insights.so_what: A shift of Strategy from perpetual net buyer to net seller of Bitcoin removes a long-standing demand pillar and is a flow-based risk factor to monitor, not just a sentiment issue.
insights.quote: Sailor has been selling Bitcoin uh to fund Stretch uh increasing his cash reserve...it's quite possible that Sailor is going to be a net seller of Bitcoin for the foreseeable future.

insights.claim: He believes the Bitcoin core developer community's lack of urgency in addressing quantum-computing resistance is a real, unhedgeable tail risk, and this — combined with the Saylor/Strategy overhang — now prevents him from ever going 'full port' (100% allocation) into Bitcoin again, unlike in prior cycles.
insights.speaker: Taiki Maeda
insights.so_what: Even a Bitcoin bull may rationally cap position sizing given an unquantifiable quantum tail risk, supporting diversification into quantum-resistant alternatives like Zcash rather than concentrated BTC exposure.
insights.quote: given the quantum situation the sailor situation even if I am bullish Bitcoin like I don't think I can ever like fullport it

insights.claim: He suggests a mutually reinforcing dynamic is possible: if Zcash rallies specifically because it is quantum-resistant while Bitcoin is not, the resulting competitive pressure could force Bitcoin developers to finally prioritize quantum resistance, meaning BTC and ZEC could end up benefiting each other rather than being purely zero-sum rivals.
insights.speaker: Taiki Maeda
insights.so_what: A ZEC rally on quantum-resistance grounds is a scenario worth watching as a potential forcing function for Bitcoin protocol development, not just a competitive threat to BTC.

insights.claim: He admits being oversized in Zcash heading into his honeymoon made him emotionally reactive and contributed to selling near the June 2026 bottom; his response has been to size future Zcash positions smaller while holding a larger cash buffer, both to reduce emotional decision-making and to preserve dry powder for future dips or new narratives.
insights.speaker: Taiki Maeda
insights.so_what: A concrete risk-management lesson: position sizing before periods of reduced attention (travel, life events) should be more conservative to avoid forced, emotion-driven exits during volatility.
insights.quote: I think one of the mistakes I made last month, um, was maybe I was oversized in the Zcash

insights.claim: He explicitly rejects single-platform 'maxi' tribalism (e.g., only holding and trading on Hyperliquid because you hold HYPE) in favor of a diversified basket across Hyperliquid, Lighter, and Variational to capture the broader perp-DEX/RWA-perps trend rather than betting on one platform winning outright.
insights.speaker: Taiki Maeda
insights.so_what: Suggests basket exposure to a sector thesis (perp DEXs) reduces single-platform risk versus concentrated bets on one winner-take-all token.

insights.claim: His stated positioning for the next 6-12 months is: long perp-DEX tokens (HYPE, LIT) and continue farming other perp platforms like Variational for airdrops, long Zcash for its unique privacy/quantum/Saylor-overhang narrative versus Bitcoin, and hold a cash buffer given his own track record of being early or wrong on timing.
insights.speaker: Taiki Maeda
insights.so_what: Summarizes his actionable multi-month allocation stance across perp-DEX tokens, Zcash, and cash for viewers tracking his positioning.
insights.quote: long perex coins and farm more perplex coins...Long Zcash given its unique positioning and overhang around Bitcoin.
